skill.name: environment-setup-guide
skill.description: Guides developers through platform-specific setup for languages, package managers, databases, Docker, environment variables, and verification steps.
skill.summary: Create practical development environment setup guides with checks, install steps, configuration notes, and troubleshooting advice.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis correctly identified many command, network, filesystem, and environment-variable examples. Review found these are documentation examples for environment setup, not hidden execution logic, but several commands such as curl-to-shell, PowerShell download execution, sudo package installation, and shell profile edits require user caution.

medium_findings.title: Installer Commands Download and Execute Remote Content
medium_findings.description: The guide includes curl-to-shell and PowerShell installer patterns. These are common setup instructions, but users could execute remote installer code without independent verification.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky installer patterns are directly present in setup examples. The context is legitimate documentation, so the risk is user execution of unverified commands rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Privileged System Changes in Setup Examples
medium_findings.description: Several examples use sudo for package installation, Docker setup, user group changes, and service checks. These commands can alter system state if copied without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sudo commands are explicit and perform expected setup tasks. They are not hidden, but they increase operational risk for users following the guide.

medium_findings.title: Environment and Secret Placeholder Guidance
medium_findings.description: The guide teaches users to create .env files and includes placeholder database URLs, API keys, and secret keys. This is normal setup guidance, but users need care to avoid committing real secrets.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly contains environment variable examples and secret placeholders. No exfiltration destination was found, so the concern is accidental exposure through generated guidance.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but review found ordinary documentation text, localhost URLs, version examples, and troubleshooting commands such as lsof.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not show cryptographic implementation or network scanning intent. The finding is kept as low severity because troubleshooting commands can still affect local processes.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Review found no text asking the auditor to ignore instructions, override policy, claim pre-approval, or skip security analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file begins with normal skill metadata and an overview. Broad review did not reveal prompt-injection language elsewhere in the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Piped to Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup guide includes commands that download scripts and execute them through a shell. Users should verify the source and prefer package-manager or checksum-verified installation paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly visible and known to be risky when copied blindly. The surrounding text frames it as an install example, not covert execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: PowerShell Download Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The Windows example downloads and executes an installer through PowerShell. This can be unsafe if the endpoint or downloaded content changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The PowerShell invocation explicitly downloads and executes content. It is a common Chocolatey install method, which lowers suspicion but not execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Profile Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide appends PATH changes to shell startup files. This is legitimate setup work, but persistent shell changes should be reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands intentionally modify user shell configuration files. They are transparent examples and do not show stealth or persistence beyond normal PATH setup.

content.value_statement: New projects often fail during setup because required tools, versions, and configuration steps are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce practical setup guides with verification and troubleshooting steps.
actual_capabilities: Identifies required languages, package managers, databases, and developer tools.
Produces setup steps for macOS, Linux, and Windows.
Adds verification commands for installed tools and running services.
Documents environment variables and example configuration files.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for common setup failures.
Drafts reusable setup scripts for project onboarding.
limitations: Commands may need updates for new tool versions or operating system changes.
Generated install commands should be reviewed before execution.
It cannot verify a real machine state unless the assistant has tool access.
It uses placeholder secrets and does not create production-ready credentials.

use_cases.title: Onboard a New Engineer
use_cases.description: Create a clear setup document that lists prerequisites, install steps, environment variables, and verification checks.

use_cases.title: Prepare a New Laptop
use_cases.description: Generate platform-specific commands for installing core tools and configuring a project workspace.

use_cases.title: Document Local Services
use_cases.description: Write Docker, database, and application startup instructions that help teams reproduce a development stack.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Setup Guide
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single project setup document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a development environment setup guide for my project. Ask for the language, operating system, package manager, database, and required tools before writing the guide.

prompt_templates.title: Cross-Platform Onboarding
prompt_templates.scenario: Team documentation for multiple operating systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write onboarding setup instructions for macOS, Linux, and Windows. Include prerequisites, install commands, configuration files, verification checks, and common troubleshooting steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Setup Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve an existing README or internal guide.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this setup documentation for missing prerequisites, unsafe commands, unclear environment variables, and missing verification steps. Suggest a cleaner version.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Reproducible Environment
prompt_templates.scenario: Build a repeatable local stack with scripts and containers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reproducible development environment for this repository. Include Docker services, version managers, setup scripts, .env.example guidance, validation commands, and rollback notes.

output_examples.input: Set up a Node.js project with PostgreSQL on macOS.
output: A prerequisite checklist for Node.js, npm, Git, PostgreSQL, and Homebrew.
Step-by-step installation and project dependency instructions.
A .env.example section with placeholder database settings.
Verification steps for Node, npm, database connectivity, and the development server.

output_examples.input: Document setup for a Python Flask app.
output: Python version requirements and virtual environment setup.
Environment variable descriptions for Flask and database configuration.
Troubleshooting notes for missing commands and connection failures.

output_examples.input: Create Docker onboarding instructions for my team.
output: Docker Desktop or engine installation guidance by platform.
Compose service descriptions for the app and database.
Commands to start, inspect, log, and stop services.
Checks that confirm containers and database connections are working.

best_practices: Review install commands before running them, especially remote scripts and sudo commands.
Use .env.example files with placeholders, and keep real secrets out of version control.
Include verification steps after each major setup phase.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys, passwords, or production connection strings into generated examples.
Do not assume one operating system or package manager works for every developer.
Do not publish setup docs without testing them on a clean environment.

faq.question: Does this skill install tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It writes guidance and commands that a user or agent can review and run.

faq.question: Can it support macOS, Linux, and Windows?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to produce platform-specific setup steps.

faq.question: Can it create .env files?
faq.answer: It can describe .env files and placeholders. Users should add real values securely.

faq.question: Is it safe to run every generated command?
faq.answer: No. Users should review commands, verify sources, and avoid running remote scripts blindly.

faq.question: Can it help debug setup failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes troubleshooting patterns for missing commands, permissions, ports, and database connections.

faq.question: Does it replace official installation documentation?
faq.answer: No. It helps organize project setup, but official docs should be checked for current commands.
